brief_summary: Three-column comparison strip of stabilizer mounting types. Column 1 shows plate-mount geometry (housing clips into plate, PCB not anchored). Column 2 shows PCB clip-in (plastic clips snap into PCB holes, tool-free). Column 3 shows PCB screw-in (threaded screws anchor housing to PCB, firmest). Each column has a two-line trade-off summary and an amber verdict line.
data_sources: stabilizers-explained.mdx § 'Types of stabilizers' — 'Plate-mount stabilizers clip into holes in the switch plate rather than attaching to the PCB'
stabilizers-explained.mdx § 'Types of stabilizers' — 'PCB clip-in stabilizers attach directly to the PCB via two plastic clips that snap into holes'
stabilizers-explained.mdx § 'Types of stabilizers' — 'PCB screw-in stabilizers use a small screw through the PCB to anchor the housing'
